skill.name: deslop
skill.description: Review a branch diff and remove AI-generated code artifacts such as unnecessary comments, abnormal defensive checks, type casts, inline imports, and inconsistent style.
skill.summary: Clean AI-generated artifacts from branch changes while preserving useful code.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: The static external command finding is partially confirmed: SKILL.md line 20 instructs the agent to run a read-only git diff command. The line 14 backtick finding and the weak cryptographic algorithm findings on lines 2 and 3 are false positives because the file contains Markdown formatting and descriptive text, not cryptographic code or shell execution.

low_findings.title: Read-Only Git Command Instruction
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 20 instructs the agent to run `git diff main...HEAD`. This is an external command, but it is read-only, uses fixed arguments, and is directly related to reviewing branch changes. No evidence found of command injection, network access, credential access, or destructive operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is explicitly present and semantically relevant to the skill. Risk is low because the command is fixed and read-only.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static False Positives
low_findings.description: Static findings for weak cryptographic algorithms at SKILL.md lines 2 and 3, and shell backtick execution at line 14, are false positives. The lines contain skill metadata and Markdown inline code text, not cryptographic operations or executable shell code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible in SKILL.md and contain no executable code. The static patterns were triggered by text context rather than dangerous behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks the agent to run `git diff main...HEAD` during review. This should be treated as a controlled read-only command and should not be expanded with untrusted branch names or user-provided shell fragments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external command is directly present in the skill instructions. The risk depends on execution context, but the documented command itself is fixed and low impact.

content.value_statement: AI-assisted changes can leave comments, casts, and defensive code that do not match the project style. This skill reviews a branch diff and removes those artifacts while preserving legitimate changes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews the current branch against main using the branch diff.
Identifies AI-style comments that do not match nearby code.
Removes abnormal defensive checks and try-catch blocks when they are unnecessary.
Flags casts to any that were added to bypass type issues.
Moves inline Python imports to the top of the file when appropriate.
Summarizes cleanup changes in one to three sentences.
limitations: Requires a Git repository with a main branch reference.
Does not prove that every removed pattern is objectively wrong.
Can miss project-specific style rules not visible in the changed files.
Should not remove defensive logic required for security or public inputs.

use_cases.title: Pre-Review Cleanup
use_cases.description: Clean a pull request before asking teammates to review it.

use_cases.title: AI Pairing Follow-Up
use_cases.description: Remove leftover AI-generated patterns after using an assistant to implement code.

use_cases.title: Style Consistency Pass
use_cases.description: Align changed files with existing project conventions before merge.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cleanup
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after completing an AI-assisted code change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use deslop on this branch. Review the diff against main and remove obvious AI-generated artifacts while preserving real behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Comment and Cast Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when changed files contain extra comments or type bypasses.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the branch for unnecessary comments and casts to any. Remove only the cases that conflict with local style or hide type problems.

prompt_templates.title: Defensive Code Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when AI added broad guards or try-catch blocks.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this branch for abnormal defensive checks and try-catch blocks. Keep security-relevant validation, but remove code that is unnecessary for trusted paths.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Diff Polish
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before opening a pull request in a mature codebase.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full deslop pass on the branch diff. Match each changed file to nearby conventions, fix inline import placement, remove AI artifacts, and summarize the final cleanup.

output_examples.input: A branch contains extra explanatory comments and broad defensive checks around trusted internal calls.
output_examples.output: Removed comments that repeated the code and simplified unnecessary guards. Kept validation that protects public input paths.

output_examples.input: A TypeScript change uses casts to any to satisfy the compiler.
output_examples.output: Replaced avoidable type bypasses with project-consistent typing. Reported the remaining type issue that needs a design decision.

output_examples.input: A Python edit includes imports inside functions without a local precedent.
output_examples.output: Moved inline imports to the top of the file and kept behavior unchanged.

best_practices: Run tests after cleanup when behavior could be affected.
Compare each change with nearby code before removing it.
Keep defensive validation for public inputs, security boundaries, and unreliable external data.
anti_patterns: Do not remove checks only because they were generated by AI.
Do not hide type issues by leaving casts to any in place.
Do not apply one style rule across files that use different local conventions.

faq.question: What does this skill remove?
faq.answer: It removes AI-style artifacts such as unnecessary comments, abnormal defensive checks, avoidable casts, inline imports, and inconsistent style.

faq.question: Does it change program behavior?
faq.answer: It is intended to preserve behavior. Review and test changes when cleanup touches logic or error handling.

faq.question: Why does it compare against main?
faq.answer: The branch diff shows what changed, so the review can focus on new artifacts instead of the whole repository.

faq.question: Can it remove security checks?
faq.answer: It should not remove checks required for public input, authorization, data validation, or external system boundaries.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is there any security concern?
faq.answer: The skill asks for a read-only git diff command. It should not run modified shell commands from untrusted text.
